etymology.description: Depicts a tool (similar to a sickle or a pair of scissors) for mowing or weeding -> used for governing -> final form 乂
etymology.interpretation: The character 乂 originally depicted a tool used for cutting, and by extension, it was associated with governing due to the notion of cutting or managing weeds as a metaphor for ruling effectively. This sense of order is crucial in governance.

examples.chinese: 这些年，他致力于地方的治理(這些年，他致力於地方的治理)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèxiē nián, tā zhìlì yú dìfāng de zhìlǐ.
examples.english: Over the years, he has dedicated himself to local governance.

examples.chinese: 为民者，先自齐以治家(為民者，先自齊以治家)。
examples.pinyin: Wèi mín zhě, xiān zì qí yǐ zhì jiā.
examples.english: Those who govern for the people, first manage their own households.

examples.chinese: 长者都希望后代能好好治理天下(長者都希望後代能好好治理天下)。
examples.pinyin: Zhǎng zhě dōu xīwàng hòudài néng hǎohǎo zhìlǐ tiānxià.
examples.english: The elders hope that future generations can govern the world well.
